page: Amazon Web Services c8i.32xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 128 vCPUs across 64 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores operating at 3.9 GHz. The system includes 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory clocked at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows top-tier multi-core performance. The instance achieves a top-tier rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU score. Its memory subsystem demonstrates strong capabilities, with read-only small block memory bandwidth reaching 10,222.53 GB/sec. Advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX are fully supported, contributing to top-tier scores in PassMark extended instructions and encryption tests. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance placing in the average tier.

The hardware is highly capable for parallel processing tasks, software compilation, and database operations, as evidenced by top-tier Geekbench compiling and PassMark database scores. It also demonstrates strong performance in LLM inference, particularly for prompt processing across small, medium, and large models. The primary architectural tradeoff is the low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2 GB per vCPU and the complete absence of local NVMe storage, which requires external storage provisioning for data-heavy workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor and powered by 128 vCPUs from 64 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing a 2.0 GB per core allocation, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. While single-core performance remains in the average tier, its multi-threaded capabilities excel in software compilation, database operations, and LLM prompt processing. This resource profile is highly efficient for compute-intensive, parallel workloads that do not require massive memory-to-CPU ratios or local disk storage, such as high-throughput web serving and distributed simulation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.32xlarge is an Intel Xeon-based compute-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized platform featuring 128 vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding parallel processing and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 128 vCPUs and 64 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores at 3.9 GHz
Equipped with 256 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Supports advanced vector and matrix extensions including AVX-512 and AMX
Achieves top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark scores
Best suited for software compilation, parallel computing, and LLM prompt processing
